description: Add DiscoveredFeature, FeatureKind, and SourceContext types to discovery.rs
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/discovery.rs
Add FeatureKind enum with variants: Skill, Agent, Hook, Plugin (derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)
Add SourceContext struct with fields: source_type: String, plugin_name: Option<String> (derive Debug, Clone)
Add DiscoveredFeature struct with fields: file_path: PathBuf, kind: FeatureKind, source_context: Option<SourceContext>, relative_path: PathBuf (derive Debug, Clone)
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo fmt --check

description: Implement discover_features() with unified recursive walk and early directory filtering
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/discovery.rs
Create pub fn discover_features(project_root: &Path, max_depth: Option<usize>) -> Result<Vec<DiscoveredFeature>, Error>
Use ignore::WalkBuilder with hidden(false), git_ignore(true), git_global(true), git_exclude(true) — same settings as discover_source_dirs()
Do NOT add the .ai/ exclusion filter — .ai/ is walked like everything else
Add filter_entry to skip known-irrelevant directories: node_modules, target, .git, vendor, __pycache__, dist, build
Apply max_depth via builder.max_depth() when provided

For each file entry in the walk, detect feature kind by file name: SKILL.md -> Skill, *.md inside agents/ parent -> Agent, hooks.json inside hooks/ parent -> Hook, aipm.toml inside .ai/<plugin>/ -> Plugin
Require both file name AND parent directory name matching to avoid false positives (e.g., SKILL.md must be inside a skills/ directory)

Classify source context from ancestor path components: /.ai/ -> source_type '.ai' with plugin_name extracted, /.claude/ -> source_type '.claude', /.github/ -> source_type '.github', otherwise -> source_context: None
Add tracing::trace! for every directory entered (dir, depth fields)
Add tracing::trace! for every directory skipped by filter (dir, reason fields)
Add tracing::trace! for every feature file detected (file, kind, source_context fields)
Add tracing::trace! on walk completion (total_features, total_dirs_walked fields)
Sort results by file_path for deterministic output
Add unit test: discovers SKILL.md in .ai/test-plugin/skills/default/SKILL.md
Add unit test: discovers SKILL.md in .claude/skills/default/SKILL.md
Add unit test: discovers SKILL.md in .github/skills/default/SKILL.md
Add unit test: discovers hooks.json in .ai/test-plugin/hooks/hooks.json
Add unit test: discovers agent .md in .ai/test-plugin/agents/reviewer.md
Add unit test: classifies source_context correctly (.ai with plugin name, .claude, .github, None)
Add unit test: respects max_depth
Add unit test: returns sorted deterministic output
Add unit test: does NOT detect SKILL.md outside a skills/ directory (e.g., docs/SKILL.md)
Add unit test: does NOT detect random .md files as agents unless inside agents/ directory
Add unit test: skips node_modules/ and target/ directories
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Add check_file() default method to Rule trait
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/rule.rs
Add fn check_file(&self, file_path: &Path, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<Vec<Diagnostic>, Error> with a default implementation that derives the parent directory and calls self.check(parent, fs)
Verify all existing rule implementations compile without changes (default method means no breakage)
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Refactor marketplace skill rules to implement check_file() accepting individual SKILL.md files
steps: Refactor skill_missing_name.rs: implement check_file() that reads and parses the single SKILL.md file directly via fs.read_to_string() and frontmatter::parse(), then checks for missing name field — no longer calls scan_skills()
Refactor skill_missing_desc.rs: implement check_file() for single SKILL.md — check for missing description field
Refactor skill_oversized.rs: implement check_file() for single SKILL.md — check file size against 15000 char limit
Refactor skill_name_too_long.rs: implement check_file() for single SKILL.md — check name length against 64 char limit
Refactor skill_name_invalid.rs: implement check_file() for single SKILL.md — check name for invalid characters
Refactor skill_desc_too_long.rs: implement check_file() for single SKILL.md — check description length
Refactor skill_invalid_shell.rs: implement check_file() for single SKILL.md — check shell value is 'bash'
Ensure each refactored rule returns the same diagnostics as before when given the same file
Update unit tests for each rule to call check_file() in addition to or instead of check()
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing tests pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Refactor agent and hook rules to implement check_file() accepting individual files
steps: Refactor agent_missing_tools.rs: implement check_file() that reads and parses the single agent .md file directly, checks for missing tools section — no longer calls scan_agents()
Refactor hook_unknown_event.rs: implement check_file() that reads the single hooks.json file directly, parses JSON, checks for unknown event names — no longer calls scan_hook_files()
Refactor hook_legacy_event.rs: implement check_file() that reads the single hooks.json file directly, checks for legacy event names — no longer calls scan_hook_files()
Refactor broken_paths.rs: implement check_file() that reads a single SKILL.md or manifest file, validates path references — keep directory-scoped context for .ai/ plugin validation
Update unit tests for each rule to call check_file()
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update MisplacedFeatures rule: remove .ai/ gate, add ai_exists field, update help text
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/misplaced_features.rs
Replace the struct fields: remove source_type and project_root, add ai_exists: bool
Remove the .ai/ existence gate at lines 49-52 (the if !fs.exists(&ai_dir) { return Ok(diagnostics); } block)
Update help_text() to branch on ai_exists: when true return 'run "aipm migrate" to move into the .ai/ marketplace', when false return 'run "aipm init" to create a marketplace, then "aipm migrate"'
Implement check_file() that produces one diagnostic per call (the engine only calls it for features NOT inside .ai/)
Update the diagnostic message format to: 'plugin feature found outside .ai/ marketplace: {path}'
Update unit test no_feature_dirs_no_finding to reflect new behavior
Update unit test skills_dir_without_marketplace_no_finding — this should NOW produce diagnostics (the bug fix)
Update unit test skills_dir_with_marketplace_warns
Update unit test github_source_type
Add new unit test: ai_exists=false produces help text mentioning 'aipm init'
Add new unit test: ai_exists=true produces help text mentioning 'aipm migrate'
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update rule registry: add quality_rules_for_kind(), deprecate for_source()
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/mod.rs
Add pub(crate) fn quality_rules_for_kind(kind: &FeatureKind) -> Vec<Box<dyn Rule>> that dispatches Skill -> 7 skill rules, Agent -> 1 agent rule, Hook -> 2 hook rules, Plugin -> 1 broken_paths rule
Add pub(crate) fn misplaced_features_rule(ai_exists: bool) -> MisplacedFeatures that constructs the rule
Mark existing for_source(), for_claude(), for_copilot(), for_marketplace() as #[deprecated] but keep them compiling for now
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Replace lint() two-phase pipeline with single-pass unified feature-based pipeline
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs
Replace the Phase 1 + Phase 2 logic in lint() with a single call to discover_features()
After discovery, apply --source filter: if opts.source is Some, retain only features whose source_context.source_type matches
Track sources_scanned from the discovered features' source_context values
Check ai_exists = fs.exists(&opts.dir.join('.ai'))
Replace run_rules_for_source() calls with a new run_rules_for_feature() function

In run_rules_for_feature(): get quality rules via quality_rules_for_kind(feature.kind), run each non-suppressed rule's check_file(), apply severity overrides, apply ignore paths, inject help_text/help_url
In run_rules_for_feature(): if feature is NOT inside .ai/, also run misplaced_features_rule(ai_exists)
In run_rules_for_feature(): if feature is inside .ai/ and kind is Plugin, run broken_paths
Add tracing::trace! for rule dispatch, rule skipped (suppressed), rule produced diagnostics, diagnostic filtered (ignore)
Keep existing diagnostic sorting (file_path -> line -> col)
Keep existing error_count/warning_count computation
Update unit test lint_empty_dir_no_sources
Update unit test lint_with_source_filter
Update unit test lint_auto_discovers_sources
Update unit test lint_discovers_nested_claude_dirs
Update unit test lint_discovers_nested_github_dirs
Update unit test lint_source_filter_claude_only
Update unit test lint_source_filter_ai_skips_discovery — this behavior changes since .ai/ is now part of unified walk
Update unit test lint_no_marketplace_no_source_findings — this should NOW produce misplaced-features diagnostics (the bug fix)
Add new unit test: quality rules run on .github/skills/default/SKILL.md (not just .ai/)
Add new unit test: quality rules run on .claude/skills/default/SKILL.md
Add new unit test: features outside any recognized source dir are detected and linted
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update CLI handler: remove .ai/-specific validation, adapt --source for unified walk
steps: Open crates/aipm/src/main.rs
In cmd_lint(), remove the special-case validation at lines 541-548 that checks .ai/ directory existence upfront (discovery handles it now)
Keep --source validation against SUPPORTED_SOURCES
Keep --max-depth threading to Options
Verify --source .ai still works (filters unified walk results to .ai/ features only)
Verify --source .claude and --source .github still work
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Add E2E tests for .github/ scenarios and cross-source quality rule execution
steps: Open crates/aipm/tests/lint_e2e.rs
Add E2E test: .github/skills/ without .ai/ produces source/misplaced-features warning
Add E2E test: .github/agents/ without .ai/ produces source/misplaced-features warning
Add E2E test: .github/skills/default/SKILL.md with missing name produces both source/misplaced-features AND skill/missing-name
Add E2E test: .claude/skills/default/SKILL.md without .ai/ produces source/misplaced-features with 'aipm init' in help text
Add E2E test: --source .github with mixed .claude and .github features only reports .github features
Add E2E test: monorepo with nested .claude/skills/ found and linted
Add E2E test: features in arbitrary directory (not .ai/.claude/.github) detected and linted
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Update discover_source_dirs() to use discover_features() internally for migrate compatibility
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/discovery.rs
Refactor discover_source_dirs() to internally call discover_features() and then map DiscoveredFeature results back to DiscoveredSource structs
Alternatively, keep discover_source_dirs() as-is but mark it deprecated and update aipm migrate to use discover_features() in a follow-up
Ensure discover_claude_dirs() still works (delegates to discover_source_dirs())
Verify all migrate tests still pass
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Run full verification gate: fmt, clippy, tests, and branch coverage >= 89%
steps: Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint violations
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' and verify TOTAL branch coverage >= 89%
If coverage is below 89%, add additional tests targeting uncovered branches in discovery.rs, misplaced_features.rs, and lint/mod.rs
Run all four checks one final time: cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace && coverage report
